title: Lateral join with correlated subquery and window function
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.top_commenter_per_post/0` that returns each post with its single most-prolific commenter (user with most comments on that post). Use a lateral join: `from p in Post, as: :post, inner_lateral_join: top in subquery(q), on: true, select: %{post_id: p.id, post_title: p.title, commenter_id: top.user_id, commenter_name: top.name, comment_count: top.cnt}`. The subquery correlates via `parent_as(:post).id == c.post_id`, groups by user_id, joins User, orders by count desc, limits to 1. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Lateral joins are a niche PostgreSQL feature that Ecto exposes. Combining with correlation via parent_as + aggregate + limit 1 is advanced.
research_citation: justinappears.com: 'Lateral joins in Ecto without fragments'
